If, at the end of summer i.e. on the full-moon day of Asadha,
where the mass of the sun's rays is screened by Mount Meru, a
northerly breeze blows, wafting the fragrance of the sweet-smelling
Kadamba flowers, then the clouds will flood the earth with water,
as though crying madly, and intoxicated by the sight of the spread-
ing illumination caused by flashes of lightning, and destroying the
Moon's rays on the earth.
[The metre is
